perturbation.original_text: Ameren Illinois disclose to Recipient, customer information protected from public disclosure pursuant to 815 ILCS 505/2HH (“Confidential Information”) for use by the Recipient and its officers, directors, agents, employees and contractors (collectively, the “Representatives”), on a need-to-know basis for the purpose of facilitating electric supply aggregation pursuant to 20 ILCS 3855/1-92.
perturbation.changed_text: Ameren Illinois may disclose to Recipient customer information for use by the Recipient and its officers, directors, agents, employees and contractors (collectively, the “Representatives”), on a need-to-know basis for the purpose of facilitating electric supply aggregation.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the reference to 815 ILCS 505/2HH eliminates the explicit requirement to protect customer information from public disclosure, potentially leading to violations of consumer privacy laws. It also removes the link to 20 ILCS 3855/1-92, which specifies how the customer information can be used.
perturbation.law_explanation: 815 ILCS 505/2HH protects customer information from public disclosure. Removing the reference to this law weakens the protection of customer information and could lead to violations of the Illinois Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Illinois General Assembly - Illinois Compiled Statutes Home Legislation & Laws Senate House My Legislation Site Map Bills & Resolutions Compiled Statutes Public Acts Legislative Reports IL Constitution Legislative Guide Legislative Glossary Search By Number (example: HB0001) Search Tips Search By Keyword Search Tips Advanced Search Illinois Compiled Statutes ILCS Listing Public Acts Search Guide Disclaimer Information maintained by the Legislative Reference Bureau Updating the database of the Illinois Compiled Statutes (ILCS) is an ongoing process. Because the statute database is maintained primarily for legislative drafting purposes, statutory changes are sometimes included in the statute database before they take effect. If the source note at the end of a Section of the statutes includes a Public Act that has not yet taken effect, the version of the law that is currently in effect may have already been removed from the database and you should refer to that Public Act to see the changes made to the current law. 815 ILCS 505/2HH (815 ILCS 505/2HH) Sec. 2HH. Billing and collection practices of electric service providers. Each person selling generation, transmission, distribution, metering, or billing of electric service shall display the name, the toll-free telephone number of such service provider, and a description of the services provided on all bills submitted to subscribers of such services. All personal information relating to the subscriber of generation, transmission, distribution, metering, or billing of electric service shall be maintained by the service providers solely for the purpose of generating the bill for such services, and shall not be divulged to any other persons with the exception of credit bureaus, collection agencies, and persons licensed to market electric service in the State of Illinois, without the written consent of the subscriber. (Source: P.A. 90-561, eff. 12-16-97.) Home | Legislation & Laws | House | Senate | My Legislation | Disclaimers | Email This site is maintained for the Illinois General Assembly by the Legislative Information System, 705 Stratton Building, Springfield, Illinois 62706 Contact ILGA Webmaster
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly references 815 ILCS 505/2HH, which, according to Scraped Law Snippet 1, mandates that personal information relating to subscribers of electric service be maintained solely for billing purposes and not divulged to other persons without written consent. The changed text removes this reference, weakening the protection of customer information. By removing the explicit reference, the modified contract fails to ensure compliance with the statute. Therefore, a legal contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Recipient is legally requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process or; in the opinion of counsel for the Recipient , by federal or state securities or other statutes, regulations or laws) to disclose any Confidential Information, the Recipient shall promptly notify Ameren Illinois of such request or requirement prior to disclosure so that Ameren Illinois may seek an appropriate protective order and/or waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Recipient is requested to disclose any Confidential Information, the Recipient may notify Ameren Illinois of such request prior to disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'legally requested or required' and changing 'shall' to 'may', the Recipient is no longer obligated to inform Ameren Illinois when legally compelled to disclose confidential information. This could violate laws requiring notification when customer data is subpoenaed or subject to legal discovery.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Illinois Financial Information Privacy Act (205 ILCS 705/8) requires financial institutions to notify customers when their financial records are requested by a government authority through a subpoena or other legal process. By removing the mandatory notification requirement, this change potentially violates this Illinois law.

perturbation.justification: The original text mandates notification to Ameren Illinois when disclosure of confidential information is legally required. The changed text removes this mandatory notification, making it optional. The Perturbation Explanation states this could violate laws requiring notification when customer data is subpoenaed or subject to legal discovery. The Law Explanation cites the Illinois Financial Information Privacy Act (205 ILCS 705/8), requiring notification when financial records are requested by a government authority. Scraped Law Snippet 1 indicates that information on 205 ILCS 705/8 is not currently available online, but the Law Explanation is sufficient to show that the change in the contract removes a legal protection required by law, which makes it a legal contradiction under the 'Omission Legal Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Ameren Illinois shall not be responsible for damages awarded due to the improper disclosure of consumer information that is adjudicated to have been caused by the Recipient’s negligence or intentional conduct.
perturbation.changed_text: Ameren Illinois shall not be responsible for damages related to the disclosure of consumer information.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the qualifier that Ameren Illinois is not responsible only if the Recipient's negligence or intentional conduct caused the improper disclosure. This could imply that Ameren Illinois is never responsible for damages related to the disclosure of consumer information, even if their own actions contributed, thus contradicting laws around data protection and consumer rights.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Illinois Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act (815 ILCS 505/2)
perturbation.law_explanation: The Illinois Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act prohibits unfair methods of competition and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in the conduct of any trade or commerce. This clause could be interpreted as an attempt to shield Ameren Illinois from liability even in cases where their actions contributed to the disclosure, which could be a deceptive practice. By implying that Ameren Illinois is never responsible for damages, the modified text contradicts this Act.

Unfair methods of competition and unfair or deceptive acts or practices, including but not limited to the use or employment of any deception fraud, false pretense, false promise, misrepresentation or the concealment, suppression or omission of any material fact, with intent that others rely upon the concealment, suppression or omission of such material fact, or the use or employment of any practice described in Section 2 of the "Uniform Deceptive Trade Practices Act", approved August 5, 1965, in the conduct of any trade or commerce are hereby declared unlawful whether any person has in fact been misled, deceived or damaged thereby. In construing this section consideration shall be given to the interpretations of the Federal Trade Commission and the federal courts relating to Section 5 (a) of the Federal Trade Commission Act. (Source: P.A. 78-904 .) Home | Legislation & Laws | House | Senate | My Legislation | Disclaimers | Email This site is maintained for the Illinois General Assembly by the Legislative Information System, 705 Stratton Building, Springfield, Illinois 62706 Contact ILGA Webmaster
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the condition that Ameren Illinois is only not responsible if the Recipient's negligence or intentional conduct caused the improper disclosure. The Illinois Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act, as shown in Scraped Law Snippet 1, prohibits unfair or deceptive acts in commerce. By implying that Ameren Illinois is never responsible, even if its own actions led to the disclosure, the modified text could violate this act, as it shields Ameren Illinois from liability even when their actions contribute to the disclosure, potentially deceiving consumers. This omission contradicts legally mandated consumer protections, as it potentially allows Ameren Illinois to engage in deceptive practices by avoiding responsibility for its own actions related to consumer information disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: Termination of this agreement may result in the suspension of information listing services provided by Ameren Illinois.
perturbation.changed_text: Termination of this agreement will result in the suspension of information listing services provided by Ameren Illinois.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'may' to 'will', the contract implies an automatic and unavoidable suspension of information listing services upon termination. If state regulations require continued access to this information under certain conditions even after termination, this clause would be in violation.
perturbation.law_explanation: 220 ILCS 5/16-117 concerns electric service provider registration and consumer protections. Under certain interpretations, some access to information listing services might be required to facilitate consumer choice even after a contract is terminated. Making the suspension mandatory could hinder that consumer choice, creating conflict with this law.

If the source note at the end of a Section of the statutes includes a Public Act that has not yet taken effect, the version of the law that is currently in effect may have already been removed from the database and you should refer to that Public Act to see the changes made to the current law. 220 ILCS 5/16-117 (220 ILCS 5/16-117) Sec. 16-117. Commission consumer education program. (a) The restructuring of the electricity industry will create a new electricity market with new marketers and sellers offering new goods and services, many of which the average consumer will not be able to readily evaluate. It is the intent of the General Assembly that (i) electricity consumers be provided with sufficient and reliable information so that they are able to compare and make informed selections of products and services provided in the electricity market; and (ii) mechanisms be provided to enable consumers to protect themselves from marketing practices that are unfair or abusive. (b) The Commission shall maintain consumer education information to help residential and small commercial retail customers understand their service options in a competitive electric services market, and their rights and responsibilities. (c) Not more than 90 days after the effective date of this amendatory Act of the 97th General Assembly, the Commission shall direct the Office of Retail Market Development to review the existing consumer education information for residential and small commercial customers and consider whether updates are necessary. The Office of Retail Market Development shall seek input from interested persons, including alternative retail electric suppliers, electric utilities, the Attorney General, and the Citizens Utility Board, to further its review of the consumer education materials and possible proposed changes. Within 4 months after the start of the review, the Office of Retail Market Development shall submit recommendations to the Commission for approval. (d) (Blank). (e) At a minimum, the consumer education information submitted to the Commission by the Office of Retail Market Development shall include concise explanations or descriptions of the following: (1) the structure of the electric utility industry following this amendatory Act of 1997 and a glossary of basic terms; (2) t
perturbation.justification: The change from 'may' to 'will' creates a legal contradiction. The original text allowed for the possibility that information listing services might not be suspended. The changed text mandates suspension. 220 ILCS 5/16-117(a) states the intent of the General Assembly is that '(i) electricity consumers be provided with sufficient and reliable information so that they are able to compare and make informed selections of products and services provided in the electricity market.' By mandating suspension of information listing services, the changed text could hinder a consumer's ability to make informed selections, creating a direct conflict with the stated intent of the law.
